notes: Spec asserts: a long-idle kiro-cli session stays alive past readyTTL, state remains ready during the idle window, and the next user prompt resumes ready->working. The recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15000ms duration_at_least_ms below cleanly exceeds the TTL; if the daemon's PID-liveness check regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (no transcript_removed, no flicker to working/waiting). The kiro-cli adapter ties session lifetime to PID liveness (ExactName{kiro-cli} + DiscoverPIDByCWD), and CheckPIDLiveness skips the readyTTL sweep for an alive PID; the fswatcher maxAge floor is 5 days, far above this idle window. observations assert kiro-cli as the agent only: kiro's JSONL carries NO model/token/cost fields (those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar the v1 adapter does not read), so model/cost/tokens are deliberately NOT asserted nonzero - they would be empty/zero and fail. pid_bind asserts the PID bound via cwd; teardown is omitted because kiro-cli process exit DELETES the session by design (final observed state is ready while alive).

text: kiro-cli TUI launches in the fresh cwd and its session appears ready
PID bound to the session via cwd match (kiro-cli parent process)
First user prompt drives ready -> working
First turn completes (text-only AssistantMessage) back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; state remains ready during the idle window while the kiro-cli PID is alive
Next user prompt: ready -> working resumes normally on the SAME session
Second turn completes back to ready; final state ready
